mechanism_type: copper-mediated aryl-aryl coupling via arylcopper or radical pathways
reactant_pattern_text: aryl halide + aryl halide or related aryl partner
common_conditions: copper source with heat or sonication, often activated Cu or Cu(I) salt
description_short: Copper-mediated homocoupling or cross-coupling of aryl halides to form biaryl C-C bonds under classical or modified Ullmann conditions.

mechanism_type: formation of Vilsmeier reagent followed by electrophilic aromatic substitution and hydrolysis
reactant_pattern_text: electron-rich arene or heteroarene + Vilsmeier reagent
common_conditions: DMF with POCl3 or related acid chloride, often 0 °C to reflux
description_short: Electrophilic formylation using a chloroiminium reagent typically generated from DMF and POCl3 to install aldehydes on electron-rich substrates.

mechanism_type: thermal or photochemical ring-opening / rearrangement via diradical or dipolar pathways
common_conditions: heat, light, Lewis acid, or metal complex activation
description_short: Strain-release rearrangement of vinylcyclopropanes into cyclopentenes under thermal, photochemical, or Lewis-acid-mediated conditions.

mechanism_type: phenol addition to β-keto ester followed by cyclization and dehydration
reactant_pattern_text: phenol + β-keto ester or equivalent carbonyl partner
common_conditions: Brønsted or Lewis acid catalysis, often heated
description_short: Acid-catalyzed condensation of phenols with β-keto esters or related partners to furnish coumarins.

transformation_type: Pd/Cu-catalyzed oxidation of alkenes to carbonyl compounds
mechanism_type: Pd(II)-mediated alkene oxidation with reoxidation by Cu/O2 or related oxidants
common_conditions: Pd catalyst with Cu co-oxidant and O2 or peroxide
description_short: Palladium-catalyzed oxidation of alkenes, especially terminal alkenes, to carbonyl compounds including methyl ketones and related oxidative cyclization products.
